title: Shakshuka Poached Eggs Tomato

seo.title: How to Make Shakshuka Poached Eggs Tomato — Easy Middle Eastern Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Shakshuka Poached Eggs Tomato. This easy Middle Eastern recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Shakshuka Poached Eggs Tomato
easy Shakshuka Poached Eggs Tomato recipe
authentic Middle Eastern Shakshuka Poached Eggs Tomato

seo.h1: Authentic Shakshuka Poached Eggs Tomato Recipe
h2Headings: What is Shakshuka Poached Eggs Tomato?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Middle Eastern Shakshuka Poached Eggs Tomato recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Shakshuka Poached Eggs Tomato — a beloved Middle Eastern classic

description: Shakshuka is a vibrant and comforting dish featuring eggs gently poached in a rich, spiced tomato sauce. This beloved Middle Eastern breakfast is bursting with flavor, perfect for a hearty start to your day. Enjoy it with warm pita bread or challah for dipping into the luscious sauce.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Heat olive oil in a large, deep skillet or cast-iron pan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and diced red bell pepper, cooking for another 3-5 minutes until the pepper starts to soften.

instructions.title: Add Spices and Tomato Paste
instructions.text: Stir in the ground cumin, smoked paprika, and chili powder (if using). Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until fragrant. This helps to toast the spices and deepen their flavor. Add the tomato paste and cook for another 2 minutes, stirring to combine.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Sauce
instructions.text: Pour in the crushed tomatoes. Season with salt and black pepper. Stir everything together, bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low. Cover the skillet and let it simmer for at least 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Create Wells for Eggs
instructions.text: Once the sauce has thickened, use the back of a spoon to create six small indentations or wells in the tomato sauce. These will be where you crack your eggs.

instructions.title: Poach the Eggs
instructions.text: Carefully crack one egg into each well. You can gently sprinkle a pinch of salt and pepper over each egg. Cover the skillet again and continue to cook on low heat for 7-10 minutes, or until the egg whites are set and the yolks are still runny (or cooked to your desired doneness).

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the skillet from the heat. Garnish generously with fresh chopped parsley and cilantro. Serve immediately, directly from the pan, with warm pita bread, challah, or crusty bread for dipping.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, consider adding a pinch of caraway seeds or a touch of smoked chili flakes to your tomato sauce.
Dont overcrowd the pan. If you have a smaller skillet, cook fewer eggs at a time or use two pans to ensure even cooking and proper poaching.
Shakshuka is best served immediately, directly from the skillet, while the eggs are still warm and the sauce is bubbling. Have your bread ready!
article: Shakshuka Poached Eggs Tomato is a beloved Middle Eastern Breakfast Recipes dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
